---
title: "3Blue1Brown: Neural Networks"
author:
  - name: ML+X Community

date: 2026-02-24
date-format: long
image: "https://img.youtube.com/vi/aircAruvnKk/maxresdefault.jpg"

categories:
  - Videos
  - Deep learning
  - First-steps
---

## About this resource
[3Blue1Brown's Neural Networks series](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLZHQObOWTQDNU6R1_67000Dx_ZCJB-3pi) is a collection of animated videos by Grant Sanderson that provides one of the most intuitive and visually compelling introductions to neural networks available online. The series covers the core ideas behind how neural networks learn, including neurons, layers, backpropagation, and gradient descent — all with the beautiful mathematical animations that 3Blue1Brown is known for.

The series is ideal for anyone who wants to build a strong visual and conceptual understanding of deep learning fundamentals before diving into code or math-heavy textbooks.

#### Key topics covered
- **Chapter 1**: But what *is* a neural network?
- **Chapter 2**: Gradient descent — how neural networks learn
- **Chapter 3**: What is backpropagation really doing?
- **Chapter 4**: Backpropagation calculus
- **Bonus**: Transformers and attention (GPT)

#### Prerequisites
- Basic familiarity with functions, derivatives, and matrix operations is helpful but not required — the visual explanations make the material accessible to beginners.

#### Estimated time to complete
The core series is approximately **1 hour** of video content. The transformer/attention bonus video adds another ~25 minutes.

## Questions?
If you any lingering questions about this resource, please feel free to post to the [Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub. We will improve materials on this website as additional questions come in.

## See also
- [**Book**: Deep Learning (Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Books/DeepLearning_Goodfellow-Bengio-Courville.html): The comprehensive deep learning textbook for a deeper mathematical treatment of the topics covered visually in this series.
- [**Book**: Understanding Deep Learning](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Books/Intro-Deeplearning_SimonJDPrince.html): A modern deep learning textbook with PyTorch code-along notebooks.
- [**Workshop**: Intro to Deep Learning with PyTorch](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-Deeplearning_PyTorch.html): Hands-on workshop to start building neural networks after watching the series.
